execution_summary: Built a Docker image (rocker/tidyverse:4.3.1 base, R 4.3.1 with dplyr) from replication_info.json, corrected the volume mount to use ./replication_data -> /app/data, and executed the R pipeline. The entrypoint k17_data_prep.R read raw Qualtrics CSVs, generated k17_processed_data.csv, and then sourced k17_analysis.R to run hierarchical regressions. I updated k17_analysis.R to save model summaries and the focal coefficient. The analysis tested the preregistered hypothesis that weekend relaxation (REQ relaxation at T2) positively predicts T3 joviality controlling for baseline and covariates. No major deviations occurred; only path/IO adjustments and result-saving code were added. The focal result was positive and statistically significant at alpha=0.05 (one-tailed).

code_executed.logs: Run completed. No stdout/stderr captured. Wrote /app/data/k17_processed_data.csv and sourced k17_analysis.R (initial run without saved summaries).
code_executed.environment: Docker image 'study26-repl' based on rocker/tidyverse:4.3.1; R 4.3.1 with base stats, dplyr

code_executed.logs: Run completed. No stdout/stderr captured. Wrote /app/data/k17_processed_data.csv and sourced k17_analysis.R, which saved /app/data/model_summaries.txt and /app/data/results_summary.csv.

results.hypothesis_tested: Among early childhood educators, weekend relaxation (REQ relaxation at T2, measured on Sunday evening) positively predicts joviality at the end of the following workweek (T3 PANAS-X joviality), controlling for baseline joviality (T1) and covariates; one-tailed alpha = 0.05.

tables.table_description: Hierarchical regression with controls, baseline T1 joviality, and weekend REQ + hassles predicting T3 joviality. Reports standardized coefficients; focal term is scale(req_relax).
Model comparison showing incremental fit when adding REQ and hassles to controls + baseline: F(5, 325) = 5.53, p = 6.65e-05.
